The classification of Gyrostachys smithii reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Zingiberaceae family.

intro: Gyrostachys smithii, also known as the "Snake Grass" or "Indian Snake Grass", is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia, belonging to the family Zingiberaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Gyrostachys smithii include glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or a powder, and is also used in the form of capsules, tablets, and syrups to take advantage of its health benefits.
